target.name: Mesh Optical Technologies
target.founder: Travis Brashears, Cameron Ramos, Serena Grown-Haeberli

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $50M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $1.0B - $11.0B.

private.name: SambaNova Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $11B

data.narrative:
Mesh Optical Technologies is a Series A-stage semiconductor company that has raised $50M to date. The valuation analysis employs a peer set of 3 private companies in the chips and semiconductors sector at Series A and Series B stages, supplemented by reference to public comparables including NVIDIA, AMD, and Altium Semiconductor.

Two methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $50M total raised as a proxy for current value and applies a 2.0x multiplier typical of Series A advancement, yielding $100M. Peer-median analysis draws from three same-sector, same/adjacent-stage companies: SambaNova Systems valued at $11.0B, Etched at $5.0B, and Positron AI at $1.0B. The median across this peer set is $5.0B, with a range of $1.0B to $11.0B.

The two methods produce a wide spread reflecting the high variance in early-stage semiconductor valuations. Triangulation yields a low-end scenario of $85M, a median of $2.5B, and a high-end scenario of $5.8B. The substantial gap between stage-progression ($100M) and peer-median ($5.0B) reflects investor expectations that frontier-chip companies can command outsized valuations relative to capital deployed. Key uncertainties include the company's differentiation within the peer set, execution risk on technology roadmap, and whether recent market resets have narrowed the valuation spread observed in the 2021-2023 cohort.
